title: I Look In Her Face (Song: Minor)
content:
I look in her face and say,
    "Sing as you used to sing
    About Love's blossoming";
    But she hints not Yea or Nay.

    "Sing, then, that Love's a pain,
    If, Dear, you think it so,
    Whether it be or no;"
    But dumb her lips remain.

    I go to a far-off room,
    A faint song ghosts my ear;
    WHICH song I cannot hear,
    But it seems to come from a tomb.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Hardy, titled "I Look In Her Face (Song: Minor)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
